metadata.title: CORR-006 — Adversarial Failure Root Cause & Proper Correction
metadata.source_guideline: ICH E5(R1) — Ethnic Factors in the Acceptability of Foreign Clinical Data
metadata.adversarial_outcome: FAILED — correction instruction contained an incorrect factual premise
metadata.resolution: Original rule retained unchanged; section_note added confirming accuracy

original_rule.text: Intrinsic ethnic factors include genetic polymorphism, age, gender, height, weight, lean body mass, body composition, organ dysfunction, race, ADME characteristics, and receptor sensitivity.
original_rule.assessment: FACTUALLY CORRECT — confirmed by direct reading of ICH E5(R1) Appendix A

correction_instruction_that_failed.instruction_claimed: ADME characteristics and receptor sensitivity are 'discussed in body text but NOT enumerated in Appendix A's factor table.' Instruction directed removal or re-attribution of these items from Appendix A to body text citation.
correction_instruction_that_failed.instruction_source_error: The correction instruction appears to have relied on the ICH E5(R1) Glossary definition of intrinsic factors (page 8), which does not enumerate ADME characteristics and receptor sensitivity. The Glossary uses a shorter, conceptual definition. However, Appendix A contains a separate, more detailed factor table that does enumerate both items explicitly.
correction_instruction_that_failed.verdict: INSTRUCTION WAS WRONG — the correction would have introduced a factual error by removing valid Appendix A citations from an accurate rule

source_verification.guideline: ICH E5(R1) — Ethnic Factors in the Acceptability of Foreign Clinical Data
source_verification.finding: Appendix A of ICH E5(R1) contains a table titled 'Intrinsic Ethnic Factors' that explicitly enumerates BOTH 'ADME characteristics' and 'Receptor sensitivity' as separate line items under the intrinsic factors category.

confusion_source.glossary_text: Intrinsic Ethnic Factors: Factors that help define and identify a sub-population; these can include genetic polymorphism, age, gender, height, weight, lean body mass, body composition, organ dysfunction, and race.
confusion_source.note: The Glossary definition (page 8) is abbreviated and does not include ADME or receptor sensitivity. Appendix A (pages 11-14) contains the full enumerated table which is the authoritative source for the complete list. The correction instruction conflated the Glossary definition with the Appendix A table.

appendix_a_intrinsic_factors_confirmed: Height / Weight / Lean body mass / Body composition

correct_rule.text: Intrinsic ethnic factors include genetic polymorphism, age, gender, height, weight, lean body mass, body composition, organ dysfunction, race, ADME characteristics, and receptor sensitivity.
correct_rule.section_note: All items including ADME characteristics and receptor sensitivity are enumerated in the Appendix A intrinsic ethnic factors table. Note: the Glossary definition (page 8) uses a shorter list and does not enumerate ADME/receptor sensitivity — this is the source of the erroneous correction instruction. Appendix A is the authoritative source for the complete factor list.
correct_rule.change_from_original: NONE — rule text unchanged. Added source_document, source_page, and section_note fields to confirm accuracy and prevent future misidentification.

lessons_learned.lesson_1: When verifying ICH guideline citations, distinguish between Glossary definitions (abbreviated, conceptual) and Appendix/Annex tables (detailed, enumerated). The two may cover different levels of detail for the same concept.
lessons_learned.lesson_2: Adversarial testing of correction instructions is essential — a correction instruction can itself contain an incorrect premise, particularly when it relies on one section of a guideline without checking the more detailed appendix.
lessons_learned.lesson_3: For E5(R1) specifically: the Glossary (page 8) and Appendix A (pages 11-14) both define intrinsic ethnic factors but at different levels of granularity. Appendix A is the authoritative enumeration for training rule purposes.
lessons_learned.impact: LOW — the original rule was correct; no patient safety or regulatory risk from retaining it unchanged. The correction instruction, if blindly applied, would have made the training data less accurate.
